name: United States (1891–1896)
description: Thirteen alternating red and white horizontal stripes (seven red, six white). In the upper hoist, a blue canton bearing 44 white five-pointed stars in six rows of 8-7-7-7-7-8.
history: Adopted on 4 July 1891 following the admission of Wyoming as the 44th state on 10 July 1890. It served as the official flag of the United States until 4 July 1896, when it was replaced by the 45-star flag after the admission of Utah.

features.symbolism: the union of the states; a new constellation
the 44 states of the Union as of 4 July 1891
